examples.title: Check Even or Odd Number
examples.description: Script to check if a number is even or odd
examples.code:
#!/bin/bash
echo "Enter a number: "
read num
if [ $((num % 2)) -eq 0 ]; then
 echo "$num is Even"
else
 echo "$num is Odd"
fi
examples.explanation: Uses arithmetic expansion to calculate remainder when divided by 2. If remainder is 0, number is even.

examples.title: File Display with Line Numbers
examples.description: Display file contents with line numbers and error handling
examples.code:
#!/bin/bash
if [ $# -eq 0 ]; then
 echo "Usage: $0 <filename>" >&2
 exit 1
fi

filename=$1

if [ ! -f "$filename" ]; then
 echo "Error: File '$filename' not found" >&2
 exit 2
fi

if [ ! -r "$filename" ]; then
 echo "Error: File '$filename' not readable" >&2
 exit 3
fi

nl -ba "$filename"
examples.explanation: Validates arguments, checks file existence and permissions, uses nl command to display with line numbers. Error messages sent to stderr.

examples.title: Menu-Driven Calculator using Case
examples.description: Simple calculator using case statement for operation selection
examples.explanation: Demonstrates case-esac for menu-driven program. Each case branch performs the selected arithmetic operation.

examples.title: For Loop - List Files
examples.description: Iterate through files and show execute permission
examples.code:
#!/bin/bash

echo "Files with execute permission:"
count=0

for file in *; do
 if [ -f "$file" ] && [ -x "$file" ]; then
 echo "$file"
 count=$((count + 1))
 fi
done

echo "Total: $count files"
examples.explanation: Uses for loop with glob pattern *. Tests each entry with -f (regular file) and -x (executable). Counter tracks total.

examples.title: Shell Function Example
examples.description: Function to find factorial of a number
examples.code:
#!/bin/bash

factorial() {
 if [ $1 -le 1 ]; then
 echo 1
 else
 prev=$(factorial $(( $1 - 1 )))
 echo $(( $1 * prev ))
 fi
}

echo "Enter a number: "
read num

if [ $num -lt 0 ]; then
 echo "Factorial not defined for negative numbers"
 exit 1
fi

result=$(factorial $num)
echo "Factorial of $num is: $result"
examples.explanation: Demonstrates recursive shell function. Function returns value via echo, captured using command substitution $().

examples.title: While Loop - Read File Line by Line
examples.description: Process file contents using while loop
examples.code:
#!/bin/bash

if [ $# -eq 0 ]; then
 echo "Usage: $0 <filename>"
 exit 1
fi

filename=$1
line=1

while IFS= read -r line_content; do
 echo "$line: $line_content"
 line=$((line + 1))
done < "$filename"
examples.explanation: Uses while read to process file line by line. IFS= prevents field splitting, -r prevents backslash interpretation. Input redirection < feeds file to loop.
